details.body: Milestone constraints are rendered twice in the prompt: once under '## Milestone Summary' (as '- Constraints: ...') and again under '## Review Policy' (as '### Milestone Plan Constraints ...'). This violates the acceptance criterion that the prompt stays concise enough for a reasonable context budget. Remove the constraints sub-list from the Milestone Summary section, keeping only the '### Milestone Plan Constraints' block under Review Policy where they serve as actionable guardrails.

In tests/cli.rs, the `project_create_from_bead_populates_prompt_with_bead_dependencies` test fixture contains two identical `if [ "$1" = "list" ] && [ "$2" = "--json" ]` blocks (lines ~1858 and ~1897). The second block is unreachable dead code because the first block exits before it. The two blocks contain different data (first includes ms-alpha.epic-1, second does not; bead-3 titles differ). Remove the second unreachable block to prevent confusion about which fixture data is actually under test.

src/cli/project.rs:296 and src/cli/project.rs:740-759: `handle_create_from_bead` currently does `load_bead_summaries(...).await.unwrap_or_default()`, which turns every `br list --json` failure into an empty map. In that state project creation still succeeds, but the generated `prompt.md` silently drops dependency status/outcome data and most planned-elsewhere enrichment, so the output no longer satisfies the new prompt contract. Propagate the `br list` error (or add an explicit deterministic fallback that still preserves the required fields) and add a regression test for the failed/missing `br list` path.

src/cli/project.rs:881-893 and src/cli/project.rs:1089-1094: when the live bead title has drifted from the milestone plan, `resolve_bead_plan` returns `matched_workstream_index = None` / `matched_bead_index = None`, and `build_planned_elsewhere_context` immediately bails out. That means a renamed bead like `ms-alpha.bead-2` loses all same-workstream planned-elsewhere context even though its implicit slot still identifies its neighbors in the plan. Preserve the 9ni.6.1 'do not confirm membership' behavior for plan_hash/plan_version, but still use the implicit slot as a non-confirming hint to recover nearby planned-elsewhere summaries.

Fix the `planned_elsewhere` location hint so it cannot use an unconfirmed title-fallback slot as if it were authoritative. In `src/cli/project.rs:884-889`, `resolve_bead_plan` records `matched_workstream_index`/`matched_bead_index` even when `membership_confirmed` is `false`. `build_planned_elsewhere_context` then prefers that unconfirmed location at `src/cli/project.rs:1121-1124`, and the neighbor scan only excludes the matched index, not the live bead id (`src/cli/project.rs:1140-1164`). With a reordered implicit plan whose old title still matches, this can add the current bead itself to `## Already Planned Elsewhere` (for example, `ms-alpha.bead-2 ... adjacent same-workstream bead`). Use only confirmed or implicit-slot-derived locations for adjacency, and/or skip any candidate whose `proposal_id == bead.id`.

Make `planned_elsewhere` summary compaction fence-safe. `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` in `src/cli/project.rs:958-964` keeps only the first non-empty line of a related bead description. If that first line is a fence opener like `````md`, `format_planned_elsewhere` injects the dangling opener verbatim into the generated prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:885-902`). Because `create_project_from_bead_context` immediately revalidates the canonical prompt shape (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:407-417`), the unclosed fence causes later `## Review Policy` / `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance` headings to disappear from validation and `project create-from-bead` fails. Strip or close fences during compaction, or choose a non-fence textual summary line instead of blindly taking the first non-empty line.

`src/cli/project.rs:1096-1108` and `src/cli/project.rs:1169-1177` should not use `infer_implicit_slot_hint` after `resolve_bead_plan` has already failed to confirm that the live bead still maps to the current plan. In the mismatched-explicit-id case, a live bead like `ms-alpha.bead-2` still matches old implicit slot 2 even after that slot has been reassigned to an explicit `ms-alpha.bead-200`, so `build_planned_elsewhere_context` proceeds to inject adjacent summaries for the wrong neighborhood. The generated prompt then says `plan_hash`/`plan_version` are untrusted but still presents `ms-alpha.bead-1` / `ms-alpha.bead-3` as nearby planned work, which is factually wrong scope guidance. Gate the fallback so it only applies when that slot is still implicit/title-fallback-safe, or drop adjacent planned-elsewhere neighbors entirely when membership is unconfirmed. Add a regression in `tests/cli.rs` for the existing mismatched-explicit-id scenario that asserts `prompt.md` does not mention those adjacent beads.

src/cli/project.rs:1096-1114 and src/cli/project.rs:1171-1220 should stop using the implicit-slot fallback to populate planned-elsewhere neighbors when the bead's membership in the current plan is unconfirmed. In the current flow, a reorder of implicit proposals can make `infer_implicit_slot_hint` anchor to an old numeric slot, and the neighbor scan then emits the title-matching proposal for the current logical bead under a different bead ID as an "adjacent same-workstream bead". That misclassifies in-scope work as planned elsewhere and can push the implementing agent away from required changes. Limit adjacent planned-elsewhere inference to confirmed membership, or explicitly filter out proposals that resolve to the current bead's title/identity before emitting them.

src/cli/project.rs:1054-1094 and src/cli/project.rs:1116-1225 should normalize ordering before rendering `upstream_dependencies`, `downstream_dependents`, and `planned_elsewhere`. Right now the prompt preserves the raw encounter order from `br show`/plan traversal, so semantically identical dependency sets can produce different `prompt.md` contents and hashes if the upstream tool returns items in a different order. Sort by a stable key such as `(id, title)` before building the prompt context.

Fix `load_bead_summaries` in `src/cli/project.rs:742-760` to accept the real `br list --json` envelope (`{"issues": [...]}`) instead of deserializing a bare `Vec<BeadSummary>`, and do not silently treat that parse failure as an empty map in `src/cli/project.rs:296-298`. The current tests stub the wrong wire format in `tests/cli.rs:1858-1893`, so in a real workspace every generated prompt falls back to `unknown` dependency/dependent status and outcome.

Thread dependency status from `br show` into the prompt builders by extending `DependencyRef` in `src/adapters/br_models.rs:227-233` and using that data in `src/cli/project.rs:1033-1097` instead of hard-coding `unknown` whenever the summary lookup misses. The fallback path locked in by `tests/cli.rs:1996-2037` currently discards status data that `br show` already returns, so any transient or partial `br list` failure still produces a non-self-contained prompt.

In `load_bead_summaries`, the `BrError::BrExitError` branch maps to `AppError::Io`, which means `br list` exit-code failures are silently swallowed by the `Err(AppError::Io(_)) => BTreeMap::new()` catch in `handle_create_from_bead`. This is the intended behavior for the `br list` failure test case. However, consider whether a `BrExitError` with stderr indicating corruption (not just 'command not found' or network failure) should also propagate rather than being silently degraded. Currently the mapping conflates transport/availability failures with application-level errors from `br list`.

`src/adapters/br_models.rs:232-235` adds `DependencyRef.status` as an `Option<BeadStatus>` without `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`. Because `DependencyRef` is still serialized and embedded in externally visible payloads such as `BeadDetail` (`src/adapters/br_models.rs:255-280`) and `BeadReadView` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/queries.rs:29-52`), every dependency/dependent that lacks a status will now start emitting `"status": null`. That is a non-prompt JSON contract change and violates the bead requirement to keep behavior outside prompt generation stable.

`src/cli/project.rs:1003-1025` computes planned-elsewhere summaries by returning the first non-empty line outside fences, and `build_planned_elsewhere_context` uses that value directly at `src/cli/project.rs:1187-1190` and `src/cli/project.rs:1262`. For structured bead descriptions like `Goal:\nKeep ...` or `Scope:\n...` (a format already supported elsewhere via `split_bead_description_scope` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:694-752`), the generated prompt will summarize the neighboring bead as just `Goal:`/`Scope:` instead of the actual scope text. That makes `prompt.md` materially less self-contained and can hide the real planned-elsewhere boundary.

Do not silently degrade prompt generation when `br list --json` fails. `handle_create_from_bead` currently converts any `AppError::Io` from `load_bead_summaries` into an empty summary map (`src/cli/project.rs:296-300`), and the downstream renderers then emit fallback/`unknown` dependency data when those summaries are missing (`src/cli/project.rs:1154-1167`, `src/cli/project.rs:1358-1366`). That means the same milestone/bead state can produce different `prompt.md` contents and different prompt hashes depending on transient `br list` availability, which violates the bead’s deterministic-rendering requirement. Either fail hard on generic `br list` failures or source the needed status/title fields from deterministic project data instead of silently dropping them.

Planned-elsewhere generation should incorporate the milestone plan’s explicit acceptance ownership, not just dependents and adjacent same-workstream beads. `build_planned_elsewhere_context` only considers `bead.dependents`, `bead.dependencies`, and +/-1 workstream neighbors (`src/cli/project.rs:1253-1376`), even though the bundle already carries authoritative `acceptance_map[].covered_by` ownership data for which beads own related milestone criteria (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:51-52`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:704-713`). As written, a related bead in another workstream with no direct dependency edge will be omitted from `## Already Planned Elsewhere`, so the prompt can miss the very planned follow-up work that is supposed to prevent scope creep and over-demanding reviews.

Fix 6 failing integration tests in tests/cli.rs: run_sync_milestone_errors_when_missing_lineage_is_ambiguous, run_sync_milestone_fails_when_completed_project_lacks_run_completed_event, run_sync_milestone_fails_when_failed_project_lacks_run_failed_event, run_sync_milestone_reconstructs_missing_lineage_for_terminal_project, run_sync_milestone_repairs_completed_bead_backed_project, run_sync_milestone_repairs_stale_terminal_outcome_with_original_timestamp. All fail with 'failed to load bead summaries: unexpected br args: list --json' because their fake br scripts don't handle `list --json`. These tests need to be updated to use `write_show_bead_script_with_default_list` (or equivalent) instead of `write_editor_script` with a show-only br stub, matching the pattern already applied to other tests in this diff.

`project create-from-bead` now hard-fails on any non-corrupt `br list --json` failure because `handle_create_from_bead` unconditionally does `load_bead_summaries(&current_dir).await?` at `src/cli/project.rs:321`, and `tests/cli.rs:2024` was flipped to lock in that behavior. That is a user-visible regression outside prompt rendering: before this change the command could still build a project from `br show` plus the milestone plan, and the override path still does not need generated prompt context at all because `create_project_from_bead_context` bypasses rendering when `prompt_override` is present (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:400`). Please restore the old best-effort fallback for transient `br list` I/O failures, and reserve hard failure for genuinely corrupt bead data.

The new shared-acceptance-owner expansion in `build_planned_elsewhere_context` (`src/cli/project.rs:1294` and `src/cli/project.rs:1336`) does not exclude direct upstream dependencies. If an acceptance criterion is jointly owned by the active bead and one of its blocking dependencies, that blocker will now be rendered again in `Already Planned Elsewhere` as `shared milestone acceptance ownership ...`, even though dependencies already have their own dedicated section in the prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:982`). That duplicates the same bead under two different semantics and re-labels prerequisite work as out-of-scope/planned-elsewhere work. Filter `upstream_ids` out of the shared-ownership pass, or merge the extra acceptance-ownership detail into the existing dependency entry instead of emitting a second planned-elsewhere item.

Tighten the `br list` fallback in `project create-from-bead`. In `src/cli/project.rs:321-325`, every `AppError::Io` from `load_bead_summaries()` is treated as recoverable and replaced with an empty summary map. That is too broad because `DependencyRef.status` is optional (`src/adapters/br_models.rs:228-235`), and `prompt_bead_status` / `prompt_bead_outcome` fall back to `"unknown"` when both the summary map and relation status are missing (`src/cli/project.rs:1164-1177`). A transient or version-skew `br list --json` failure can therefore still create `prompt.md` with `unknown` dependency status/outcome, which violates the bead contract's requirement that dependency status be rendered. Narrow the fallback to the explicitly supported unsupported-list case, or refuse to continue unless every dependency/dependent already carries a status; add a regression test for `br list` failure with `br show` responses that omit relation statuses (the current fallback test at `tests/cli.rs:2039-2071` only covers status-rich `show` payloads).

In build_planned_elsewhere_context, when a bead is both a downstream dependent and a shared acceptance owner, the shared acceptance relationship info (criterion IDs) is silently dropped by the seen_ids dedup. Consider either: (a) merging the acceptance criterion info into the dependent's relationship label when both apply, or (b) explicitly filtering dependent_ids in the shared_acceptance_owners loop (like upstream_ids is filtered) and documenting that the dependent relationship takes precedence.

Avoid forcing the new `br list` prompt-hydration path when the user supplies `--prompt-file`. `handle_create_from_bead` still unconditionally calls `load_bead_summaries` and builds the enriched prompt context at `src/cli/project.rs:310-354`, but `create_project_from_bead_context` discards that generated context as soon as `prompt_override` is present at `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:400-405`. That means `project create-from-bead --prompt-file ...` can now fail on `br list` errors or missing relation-status metadata that are irrelevant to the override prompt, which is a regression outside prompt generation. Short-circuit the summary/planned-elsewhere loading in the override path, or at minimum make those failures non-fatal when the override is used.

Teach planned-elsewhere summary compaction to ignore level-1 markdown headings. The helper used by `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` only recognizes headings with at least two `#` characters at `src/cli/project.rs:1069-1075`, so a related bead description that starts with a valid `# Heading` falls through and gets emitted verbatim as the summary at `src/cli/project.rs:1153-1170`. In that case the prompt shows `Summary: # Heading` instead of the actual scope text, which weakens the new self-contained planned-elsewhere context. Use a heading check that skips any ATX heading level for this compaction path.

In `src/adapters/br_process.rs:168-170` / `src/cli/project.rs:766-780`, stop hydrating `bead_summaries` from bare `br list --json`. `build_planned_elsewhere_context()` depends on that map for related-bead titles/statuses (`src/cli/project.rs:1396-1420`, `src/cli/project.rs:1474-1482`), but bare `br list` only returns the first 50 open issues and excludes closed/deferred ones. That means closed, deferred, or paged-out adjacent/shared-acceptance beads will be rendered with `status: unknown` or incomplete enrichment even though the data exists, which breaks the self-contained prompt contract and can mis-scope follow-on work. Fetch an exhaustive set (`--all --deferred --limit=0`) or query the specific related IDs instead, then add a regression test that covers a closed adjacent bead in a repo with >50 open issues.

High: `project create-from-bead` can now hard-fail on ordinary `br list` filesystem errors instead of taking the intended deterministic fallback. In `src/cli/project.rs:321-333`, generic `AppError::Io` from `load_bead_summaries` is supposed to degrade to direct relation metadata when statuses are present, but `map_br_list_error` + `br_list_exit_error_looks_corrupt` (`src/cli/project.rs:784-822`) classify any stderr mentioning `.beads/issues.jsonl` / `issues.jsonl` as `CorruptRecord`. A missing-file or permission-denied message on `.beads/issues.jsonl` will therefore bypass the fallback and abort project creation even though the prompt could still be rendered deterministically from `br show` relation statuses.

Medium: planned-elsewhere summaries mishandle valid bead-description section labels and can emit useless summaries like `Non-goals:` instead of real scope text. `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` only strips a small whitelist of labels in `src/cli/project.rs:1113-1127` and otherwise returns the first non-empty line in `src/cli/project.rs:1182-1195`. But the prompt system already recognizes plain `Non-goals:` / `Acceptance Criteria:` section labels as legitimate bead-description boundaries in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:520-537`. If a neighboring bead description starts with one of those supported labels, the generated `Already Planned Elsewhere` section becomes misleading instead of self-contained.

Medium: the new milestone progress string double-counts unfinished beads and misstates milestone state. `render_bead_task_prompt` formats progress as `... in progress; ... blocked; ... remaining` using `MilestoneProgress::remaining()` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:844-854`, but `remaining()` is defined in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:221-226` as `total - completed - failed - skipped`, which already includes in-progress and blocked beads. That means prompts can report combinations like `1/4 completed; 1 in progress; 1 blocked; 3 remaining`, which is internally inconsistent and can mislead the agent reading only `prompt.md`.

Handle level-1 markdown headings when extracting bead-local `Non-goals` and `Acceptance Criteria` from descriptions. In `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:501-537` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:694-751`, `markdown_heading_title()` ignores `# ...` headings (`hash_count < 2`), so a valid bead description like `# Non-goals` / `# Acceptance Criteria` is left inside `Must-Do Scope` instead of being extracted into the canonical sections. That makes `prompt.md` misstate scope and can drop acceptance criteria entirely on otherwise normal markdown input. Update the parser to recognize level-1 through level-6 headings consistently and add a regression test covering `# Non-goals` and `# Acceptance Criteria`.

Fix the unconfirmed-membership path in `build_planned_elsewhere_context` (`src/cli/project.rs:1357-1450`). The function correctly suppresses same-workstream neighbor inference only after the later `location_hint` gate (`src/cli/project.rs:1430-1450`), but it still eagerly derives planned-elsewhere entries from `bundle.acceptance_map` and `proposal_lookup` beforehand (`src/cli/project.rs:1357-1427`). When `resolve_bead_plan` left `membership_confirmed=false` because the live bead only matched by title fallback, those plan lookups are no longer trustworthy: implicit bead IDs may now refer to a different proposal, so the generated prompt can incorrectly mark unrelated work as "already planned elsewhere" or attach the wrong plan summary. I reproduced this with a renamed live `ms-alpha.bead-2`: the prompt still included `ms-alpha.bead-4 ... shared milestone acceptance ownership in Validation (AC-1)` even though the bead/plan mapping was explicitly unconfirmed. Guard all plan-derived planned-elsewhere enrichment behind a confirmed membership check (or another proof that the referenced IDs are explicit/stable), and add a regression test for the renamed-live-bead/shared-acceptance case.

Relax the `br list` fallback in `project create-from-bead` so missing relation statuses do not abort project creation. Right now `src/cli/project.rs:321-333` calls `ensure_direct_relations_have_statuses_for_prompt`, and `src/cli/project.rs:849-874` turns any missing inline status into a hard error whenever `br list --all --deferred --limit=0 --json` is unavailable. That is stricter than the renderer itself needs, because the prompt helpers already have deterministic `unknown` fallbacks in `src/cli/project.rs:1222-1262`. Amend this path to keep generating the prompt with `unknown` status/outcome instead of failing the whole command.

Add a deterministic size budget for planned-elsewhere context before rendering `prompt.md`. `src/cli/project.rs:1321-1502` currently accumulates every direct dependent, every shared-acceptance owner, and adjacent neighbors, and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:895-916` plus `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1021` render the entire collection verbatim. There is no cap on item count or summary length, so large milestones can produce unbounded prompt growth. Amend this by ranking items deterministically and enforcing a fixed max-items/max-bytes budget before serialization.

In `planned_elsewhere_serialized_bytes` and `planned_elsewhere_serialized_bytes_without_summary` (project.rs:1563-1580), the byte estimation does not account for the bullet prefix '- ' (2 bytes per item), inter-item newline separators from `bullet_lines` join (1 byte per item except last), or the 4-space continuation indent added by `render_bullet_item` for each continuation line in multi-line summaries. This means the actual rendered output in `format_planned_elsewhere` (service.rs:895-917) will consistently exceed the tracked budget. For 6 max items with multi-line summaries, the undercount could reach ~100-140 bytes (~7-9% of the 1536-byte budget). Consider either: (a) adding the bullet/join overhead to the byte estimation (e.g., +3 bytes per item for '- ' and '\n'), or (b) documenting that the budget is an intentional approximation of content bytes excluding formatting chrome, so the relationship between the constant and actual output is understood by future maintainers.

Cap or summarize the dependency section before rendering `prompt.md`. Right now `render_bead_task_prompt` prints every upstream dependency and downstream dependent verbatim (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:867-893`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:991-1001`), while only planned-elsewhere items are budgeted (`src/cli/project.rs:1499-1525`). For beads with large fan-in/fan-out or epic-style child relationships, the generated prompt can grow without bound and violate the bead's explicit context-budget requirement.

Make `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` preserve continuation lines from the same paragraph instead of returning only the first non-empty line (`src/cli/project.rs:1145-1184`). As written, any hard-wrapped milestone-plan description is truncated at the first newline, so nearby-bead summaries can lose the qualifying text that tells the agent what is actually planned elsewhere.

In `format_dependency_context` (service.rs lines 942-958), the `omission_line` text is computed once at line 947 with the omitted count based on `selected.len()` at that point. The subsequent `while` pop-loop (lines 948-956) removes items from `selected` to make room for the omission line, but never recomputes the omission text. After pops, the displayed count is lower than the actual number of omitted items (e.g., text says '15 omitted' when 17 are actually omitted). Additionally, the stale text is shorter than the correct text, so the budget check may accept an output that is slightly over-budget with the correct text. Fix: recompute `omitted_dependency_context_line(rendered_items.len() - selected.len(), label)` inside the pop-loop after each `selected.pop()`.

`src/cli/project.rs:1556-1598` can silently drop real planned-elsewhere items when the base line (`id (title) - relationship; status: ...`) alone exceeds the remaining budget. `fit_planned_elsewhere_item_to_budget` returns `None` in that case, `apply_planned_elsewhere_budget` skips the item, and `render_bead_task_prompt` later renders an empty list as `No explicit planned-elsewhere items were supplied.` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:961-964`). A single very long bead title, workstream name, or shared-acceptance relationship can therefore erase genuine planned-elsewhere scope and tell the agent there is none. The budgeter needs to preserve at least a truncated base line or emit an omission marker instead of dropping the item entirely.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:907-958` drops all concrete dependency details when the first rendered dependency line is longer than `DEPENDENCY_CONTEXT_MAX_BYTES`. In that case the selection loop leaves `selected` empty and returns only an omission bullet like `1 additional upstream dependencies omitted for prompt budget.` That means the prompt can contain no actual dependency id/status/outcome at all, even for a single upstream blocker or downstream dependent with a long title. The renderer should always keep at least the dependency id and statuses, truncating long titles/relationship text instead of replacing the whole section with an omission count.

In `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` lines 931-938, `truncate_to_char_boundary` returns the first character even when it exceeds `max_bytes` (e.g., a 4-byte emoji when max_bytes=2). The `project.rs` version (lines 1734-1744) correctly returns `""` when `first_char_end > max_bytes`. The service.rs version should match: replace `return &value[..first_char_end.min(value.len())];` with the same `if first_char_end <= max_bytes { &value[..first_char_end] } else { "" }` guard used in project.rs. Without this fix, `truncate_with_ascii_ellipsis` in service.rs can produce strings exceeding the requested byte limit when input contains multi-byte characters at the start, causing fitted dependency items to silently exceed the budget.

The functions `truncate_with_ascii_ellipsis` and `truncate_to_char_boundary` are now fully duplicated between `src/cli/project.rs` (lines 1706-1748) and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` (lines 903-941), with slightly different edge-case behavior (the service.rs version has the bug above). Extract these into a shared utility module so both call sites use the same implementation and stay in sync.
